title: Pterocarpus pedatus
intro: Pterocarpus pedatus, commonly known as the red beech, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Africa, to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Pterocarpus pedatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pterocarpus pedatus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a powder or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Pterocarpus pedatus belongs to the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This family is one of the largest and most widespread groups of flowering plants. The genus Pterocarpus is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Pterocarpus pedatus is a species within this genus and is part of the order Fabales. The classification of Pterocarpus pedatus is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Fabales, Family: Fabaceae, Genus: Pterocarpus, Species: P. pedatus.
